comments.text:
As a clinician, I deeply resonate with this. No physician chooses medicine to spend hours navigating documentation systems; yet administrative load is silently reshaping clinical attention and cognitive bandwidth.

The real question is not whether AI can document faster, but whether it can do so with explainability, interpretability, and transparency at a level that preserves clinical accountability.

In high stakes environments like healthcare, workflow AI must go beyond productivity:
• How are clinical suggestions generated?
• Are reasoning pathways auditable?
• Can outputs be traced back to structured evidence rather than probabilistic abstraction?

From a security architecture standpoint, I’m also curious:
Are model inferences processed within a zero trust framework with strict PHI boundary enforcement, clear on device vs. cloud segregation, and differential privacy or federated safeguards?

Clinician trust will ultimately depend not only on usability, but on epistemic clarity and data security maturity.

How do we balance workflow automation with deep clinical interpretability?
